req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-003 specifies that customers may transfer up to $25,000 per day between own accounts; transfers exceeding this limit must be declined with 422. The test suite covers: (1) a positive case within the daily limit to verify successful atomic transfer; (2) a negative case exceeding the daily limit to verify rejection; (3) a boundary case at exactly $25,000 to verify the inclusive upper bound; and (4) an additional negative case for insufficient funds (409). These tests apply Equivalence Partitioning (valid vs. invalid amounts), Boundary Value Analysis (at and above the $25,000 threshold), and Negative Path analysis (error conditions). All tests are deterministic, idempotent, and self-contained using parameterized fixtures.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer account A exists with balance >= $10,000
Customer account B exists, owned by same customer
No transfers have been made today for this customer
expected: Source account A balance decreased by $10,000
Target account B balance increased by $10,000
Transaction is atomic (both debit and credit present)
trace: R-FIN-003: Transfer amount $10,000 is within $25,000 daily limit
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_base, customer_token, acct_a_id, acct_b_id):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": acct_a_id,
        "target_account_id": acct_b_id,
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {customer_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201], f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("status") == "completed"
    assert data.get("amount") == 10000
    resp_a = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_a_id}", headers=headers)
    resp_b = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_b_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_a.json().get("balance") == 90000
    assert resp_b.json().get("balance") == 10000

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_rejected
preconditions: Customer account A exists with balance >= $30,000
Customer account B exists, owned by same customer
No transfers have been made today for this customer
expected: Error code indicates daily_limit_exceeded or similar
Source and target account balances unchanged
trace: R-FIN-003: Transfer amount $26,000 exceeds $25,000 daily limit
Error pathway: 422 when daily limit exceeded
Accounts remain locked to prevent partial execution
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_rejected(api_base, customer_token, acct_a_id, acct_b_id):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": acct_a_id,
        "target_account_id": acct_b_id,
        "amount": 26000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {customer_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    error_data = resp.json()
    assert "daily_limit" in error_data.get("error_code", "").lower()
    resp_a = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_a_id}", headers=headers)
    resp_b = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_b_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_a.json().get("balance") == 100000
    assert resp_b.json().get("balance") == 0

tests.name: test_transfer_at_daily_limit_boundary_succeeds
preconditions: Customer account A exists with balance >= $25,000
Customer account B exists, owned by same customer
No transfers have been made today for this customer
actions: POST /transfers/internal with source=A, target=B, amount=$25,000 (exactly at limit)
expected: Source account A balance decreased by exactly $25,000
Target account B balance increased by exactly $25,000
trace: R-FIN-003: Boundary Value Analysis at upper inclusive bound ($25,000)
Requirement states 'up to $25,000' meaning $25,000 is included
Expected outcome: transfer accepted and atomic
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_transfer_at_daily_limit_boundary_succeeds(api_base, customer_token, acct_a_id, acct_b_id):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": acct_a_id,
        "target_account_id": acct_b_id,
        "amount": 25000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {customer_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201], f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("status") == "completed"
    assert data.get("amount") == 25000
    resp_a = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_a_id}", headers=headers)
    resp_b = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_b_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_a.json().get("balance") == 75000
    assert resp_b.json().get("balance") == 25000

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_rejected
preconditions: Customer account A exists with balance = $5,000
Customer account B exists, owned by same customer
expected: Source and target account balances unchanged
trace: R-FIN-003: Error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
Precondition check: source balance < requested amount
Transfer rejected before execution to maintain data integrity
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_rejected(api_base, customer_token, acct_low_balance_id, acct_b_id):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": acct_low_balance_id,
        "target_account_id": acct_b_id,
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {customer_token}"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{api_base}/transfers/internal", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}"
    error_data = resp.json()
    assert "insufficient" in error_data.get("error_code", "").lower()
    resp_src = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_low_balance_id}", headers=headers)
    resp_tgt = requests.get(f"{api_base}/accounts/{acct_b_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_src.json().get("balance") == 5000
    assert resp_tgt.json().get("balance") == 0
